title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
summary: Max Ryabinin from Together AI presents techniques for training LLMs with extremely long context windows (3-5 million tokens) on limited hardware. The approach stacks fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. The novel contribution, Untied Ulysses, further optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention head groups and reusing buffers across iterations to reduce activation memory while maintaining throughput.
writeup: Max Ryabinin of Together AI walks through the stack of techniques needed to train LLMs with very long context windows: fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and chunked sequence training. The core contribution is Untied Ulysses, which optimizes context parallelism by chunking attention heads and reusing buffers across iterations. These methods together enable 5 million token context training at 8B and 32B parameter scale.
